concerns.title: No Dockerfile for FabricService
concerns.description: Dockerfile template exists in src/Docker.md but it's not a standalone Dockerfile file — lacks requirements.txt and CMD uses uvicorn main:app while main.py does not define app
concerns.impact: Cannot deploy fabric in containerized environment; docker build fails without requirements.txt and main:app
concerns.proposed_solution: Create actual Dockerfile in root: Python 3.11 slim → install dependencies from requirements.txt → copy src/ and fix CMD to run main.py via asyncio
concerns.evidence: src/Docker.md:1-31 contains non-executable Dockerfile
concerns.notes: CMD needs to use python -m main or create an ASGI app object

concerns.title: No docker-compose.yml
concerns.description: docker-compose.yml template only exists inside src/Docker.md but not as a standalone file in the root — no ability to run dependent services
concerns.impact: Integration tests cannot be run and developer needs to manually create full topology
concerns.proposed_solution: Create actual docker-compose.yml with fabric-service, redis, optional actor_service
concerns.evidence: src/Docker.md:36-67 contains non-usable compose
concerns.notes: Needs DEV-001 executable Dockerfile first

concerns.title: No CI/CD Pipeline
concerns.description: No GitHub Actions, Jenkinsfile, or any CI exists — no automation for testing or deployment
concerns.impact: Any merge happens without tests — zero quality assurance and risk of breaking production
concerns.proposed_solution: Create GitHub Actions workflow: lint → typecheck → unit tests → integration tests → build image
concerns.notes: Needs DEV-001 and DEV-007 first

concerns.title: No Runtime Configuration Schema
concerns.description: No schema for YAML configuration — transport_example.yaml is good but any wrong config causes silent runtime failure that cannot be detected before runtime
concerns.impact: Configuration errors only detected at runtime with no pre-validation
concerns.proposed_solution: Create Pydantic schema for each config and JSON Schema for YAML; add ConfigValidator before load_config
concerns.evidence: src/wiring/config/loader.py, src/wiring/config/transport_example.yaml

concerns.title: Partial Graceful Shutdown / Signal Handling
concerns.description: AppRunner in runner.py implements full signal handlers (SIGINT/SIGTERM) with Windows fallback and reverse close via FabricClient.close() — but main.py does not import AppRunner making graceful shutdown inaccessible
concerns.impact: Unclean shutdown — data loss, stuck sessions, resource leaks because main.py lacks AppRunner import
concerns.proposed_solution: Add from wiring.runner import AppRunner to main.py to link existing implementation to entry point
concerns.evidence: src/wiring/runner.py:62-78 implements handlers; src/main.py:23 missing import
concerns.notes: runner.py is ready — issue is only in main.py import

concerns.title: No Versioning Strategy for Internal APIs
concerns.description: No strategy to document and version the ICommunicationGateway contract — no __version__ or CHANGELOG
concerns.impact: Contract changes break all consumers without warning or migration period
concerns.proposed_solution: Add __version__ to contracts and document breaking changes in CHANGELOG
concerns.evidence: src/wiring/contracts/communication_gateway.py

concerns.title: Missing requirements.txt
concerns.description: No file to install dependencies — Docker.md assumes requirements.txt exists but the file is completely missing from the project
concerns.impact: Prevents final Docker image build, breaks package installation, and breaks any CI/CD environment
concerns.proposed_solution: Create requirements.txt listing actual project dependencies (asyncio, PyYAML, etc.)
concerns.evidence: Docker.md:9 references COPY requirements.txt but it does not exist
concerns.notes: Blocker for DEV-001, DEV-002, DEV-003

concerns.title: Missing pyproject.toml / setup.py
concerns.description: No Python package file exists — project cannot be installed as package or deployed or version managed
concerns.impact: Cannot pip install -e . or deploy as package or run tests easily with pytest
concerns.proposed_solution: Create pyproject.toml with metadata and dependencies using setuptools or poetry

concerns.title: Broken AppRunner import in main.py
concerns.description: main.py calls AppRunner.run() on line 23 but AppRunner is not imported — causes immediate NameError on execution
concerns.impact: Application does not work on run — NameError prevents service startup entirely
concerns.proposed_solution: Add from wiring.runner import AppRunner to top of main.py
concerns.evidence: src/main.py:23 uses AppRunner without import
concerns.notes: Must be solved with DEV-005

concerns.title: No .gitignore
concerns.description: No .gitignore exists — __pycache__ and .pyc files are pushed to repo causing repo bloat
concerns.impact: .pyc files and __pycache__ directories appear in repo causing noise in reviews and unnecessary diffs
concerns.proposed_solution: Create .gitignore that ignores __pycache__/ and *.pyc and .env and .venv and *.egg-info
concerns.evidence: glob results show .pyc files in repo

concerns.title: No FabricService (FastAPI Gateway App)
concerns.description: No FastAPI app that combines FastAPIProtocolRouter + FabricHealthEndpoints into a runnable Gateway Mode service with endpoints: POST /send, /receive, /stream, /close_session, GET /live, /ready, /health
concerns.impact: Gateway Mode cannot run — one of two main deployment modes completely missing
concerns.proposed_solution: Create src/application/fabric_service.py that creates a FastAPI() app, includes FastAPIProtocolRouter (with /send, /receive, /stream, /close_session routes) and FabricHealthEndpoints (with /live, /ready, /health). Wire it into main.py as an optional entry-point.
concerns.evidence: src/application/fastapi_router.py has FastAPIProtocolRouter with /v1/{path}, /v1/graphql, /v1/webhooks/{event_type}, /v1/stream but NO FastAPI app wraps it. src/wiring/adapters/fabric_health.py has FabricHealthEndpoints with /live, /ready, /health but is not wired into any FastAPI app. .docs/usage_integration_examples/tests/test_fabric_integration.py assumes a running FabricService at localhost:8000.
concerns.notes: Replace GWY-001 scope: individual building blocks (FastAPIProtocolRouter, FabricHealthEndpoints) DO exist, but the aggregating FastAPI application is missing.

concerns.title: No RemoteGatewayAdapter in src/
concerns.description: RemoteGatewayAdapter (httpx.AsyncClient-based ICommunicationGateway) exists only in .docs/usage_integration_examples/gateway_mode/remote_adapter.py, not in src/
concerns.impact: Gateway Mode cannot run — actor process has no way to connect to remote FabricService
concerns.proposed_solution: Copy .docs/usage_integration_examples/gateway_mode/remote_adapter.py -> src/application/remote_adapter.py; add liveness_check/readiness_check to ICommunicationGateway contract
concerns.evidence: gateway_mode/remote_adapter.py imports from contracts.communication_gateway but .docs is NOT importable from src/ without sys.path manipulation
concerns.notes: Also missing: liveness_check() and readiness_check() are called by RemoteGatewayAdapter but NOT defined in ICommunicationGateway protocol

concerns.title: No Client SDK Package
concerns.description: No pip-installable package (pyproject.toml/setup.py) for FabricClient, ICommunicationGateway, or RemoteGatewayAdapter
concerns.impact: Every fabric user must copy-paste contracts and adapters manually; imports from .docs/break unless sys.path is manipulated
concerns.proposed_solution: Create pyproject.toml with fabric-sdk package; export ICommunicationGateway, FabricClient, RemoteGatewayAdapter as public API; add proper __init__.py exports
concerns.evidence: No pyproject.toml, setup.py, setup.cfg, or requirements.txt found anywhere in the project. README instructs 'pip install -r requirements.txt' which does not exist.

concerns.title: No Formal API Versioning Scheme
concerns.description: No documented versioning strategy; /v1 prefix exists ad-hoc in FastAPIProtocolRouter but there is no version policy, no Accept-Version header, and no deprecation/ migration docs
concerns.impact: Once FabricService goes to production, any contract change breaks all connected clients without warning
concerns.proposed_solution: Define versioning strategy doc; add /api/v1 prefix consistently; add optional Accept-Version header support; document breaking-change policy in ARCHITECTURE.md or equivalent
concerns.evidence: src/application/fastapi_router.py uses prefix='/v1' by default (line 65). gateway_mode/actor_service/main.py uses http://fabric-service:8000/api/v1. No formal version enforcement exists.

concerns.title: No OpenAPI API Documentation
concerns.description: No auto-generated OpenAPI docs because no FastAPI() app exists to aggregate routers; even individual routers lack Pydantic response models
concerns.impact: Adopting Gateway Mode requires reading source code directly — no interactive API docs
concerns.proposed_solution: Create FastAPI app in fabric_service.py with include_router; add Pydantic request/response models for each endpoint; enable FastAPI auto-generated /docs and /redoc
concerns.evidence: FastAPIProtocolRouter returns Response/StreamingResponse/JSONResponse without Pydantic models. FabricHealthEndpoints returns dicts without Pydantic models.

concerns.title: No Client-Side Retry or Circuit Breaking in RemoteGatewayAdapter
concerns.description: RemoteGatewayAdapter (.docs only) has timeout=30.0 but zero retry logic; L4 RetryEngine and AsyncCircuitBreaker exist in src/ but are internal mechanisms, not exposed to the SDK client
concerns.impact: If FabricService is busy or briefly unavailable, the actor fails immediately without resilience
concerns.proposed_solution: Add RetryDecorator or httpx mounts with retry transport to RemoteGatewayAdapter; optionally expose AsyncCircuitBreaker for gateway connection health
concerns.evidence: src/transport/retry.py has RetryEngine with exponential backoff. src/network/resilience/circuit_breaker.py has AsyncCircuitBreaker. Neither is used by RemoteGatewayAdapter (.docs).
concerns.notes: Internal retry/breaker are L4-L5 mechanisms; gateway SDK needs its own client-side resilience layer

concerns.title: src/main.py missing AppRunner import
concerns.description: src/main.py line 23 calls 'await AppRunner.run(config_file)' but AppRunner is never imported — this is a runtime NameError waiting to happen
concerns.impact: Cannot run python src/main.py — entry point is broken by design
concerns.proposed_solution: Add 'from wiring.runner import AppRunner' to src/main.py imports
concerns.evidence: src/main.py:23 uses AppRunner, but there is no import statement for it (verified via grep — zero matches for 'from wiring' or 'import.*AppRunner' in main.py)
concerns.notes: This is a blocking bug in the composition root

concerns.title: No src/contracts/ directory — import path mismatch
concerns.description: README, conftest.py, and usage examples import from 'contracts.communication_gateway' but actual file is at 'src/wiring/contracts/communication_gateway.py'. SDK consumers cannot import without knowledge of internal layout.
concerns.impact: Consumers get ImportError; the public API path is undocumented and inconsistent with the codebase's own tests and docs
concerns.proposed_solution: Either (a) add src/contracts/__init__.py with re-exports from wiring.contracts, or (b) update all .docs references to use 'from wiring.contracts.communication_gateway import ICommunicationGateway'
concerns.evidence: conftest.py:14: 'from contracts.communication_gateway import ICommunicationGateway'. README docs show top-level contracts/ dir. But actual path is src/wiring/contracts/
concerns.notes: The .docs/ directory works via implicit sys.path but src/ does not have this re-export

concerns.title: ICommunicationGateway protocol missing liveness_check/readiness_check
concerns.description: ICommunicationGateway defines only health_check(). Both FabricHealthEndpoints and RemoteGatewayAdapter call liveness_check() and readiness_check() which are not in the protocol contract
concerns.impact: Gateway health probes and remote adapter break structural subtyping; any ICommunicationGateway implementation must add extra methods without contract enforcement
concerns.proposed_solution: Add 'async def liveness_check() -> Dict[str, Any]: ...' and 'async def readiness_check() -> Dict[str, Any]: ...' to ICommunicationGateway Protocol class
concerns.evidence: src/wiring/adapters/fabric_health.py:56-62 calls self.gateway.liveness_check() and self.gateway.readiness_check(). src/wiring/contracts/communication_gateway.py:45 only defines health_check. .docs/gateway_mode/remote_adapter.py:50-51 implements liveness_check/readiness_check outside the protocol.

concerns.title: CircuitBreaker Not Wired
concerns.description: AsyncCircuitBreaker exists and is built via build_circuit_breaker but is not used or injected into any channel_pool or router — additionally, record_success()/record_failure() are private methods (not public) making external integration impossible
concerns.impact: No protection from cascade failures — a single channel failure can overwhelm the system; external integration impossible due to lack of public record methods
concerns.proposed_solution: Add public record_success and record_failure methods to AsyncCircuitBreaker; wire CircuitBreaker around AsyncChannelPool.acquire(); call .record_success()/.record_failure() after each send in the upper layer; modify build_channel_pool to accept optional circuit_breaker
concerns.evidence: network/resilience/circuit_breaker.py:45-58; wiring/factories/network_provider.py build_circuit_breaker unused; circuit_breaker._record_success() and _record_failure() are private and cannot be called externally

concerns.title: DNSResolver Not Used by Assembler
concerns.description: StandardDnsResolver is built via factory but CommunicationFabricAssembler does not use it — endpoint resolution is disconnected
concerns.impact: service discovery ineffective — endpoints are hardcoded in config and not resolved dynamically
concerns.proposed_solution: Inject dns_resolver into SessionRouterAdapter so any missing channel gets DNS-resolved before failure
concerns.evidence: network/adapters/dns_resolver.py exists; wiring/factories/network_provider.py exports DNSResolver but not used

concerns.title: ConfigResolver Not Used by Assembler
concerns.description: ConfigServiceResolver is built but not wired — services defined in YAML do not actually affect routing
concerns.impact: YAML service configuration does not actually affect routing — discrepancy between docs and reality
concerns.proposed_solution: Inject config_resolver into SessionRouterAdapter as fallback source for endpoints
concerns.evidence: network/adapters/config_resolver.py exists; wiring/factories/network_provider.py exports ConfigResolver but unused

concerns.title: LoadBalancer Strategies Untested
concerns.description: RoundRobinStrategy and LeastActiveStrategy have select() functionality but no tests with real channel pool — plus RoundRobinStrategy has lock (asyncio.Lock()) that is never used
concerns.impact: Cannot guarantee correct load distribution under concurrency; unused lock causes maintenance confusion
concerns.proposed_solution: Write tests: (1) RoundRobin distributes evenly across 3 channels (2) LeastActive selects least loaded (3) unhealthy channel filtering (4) remove unused lock or use it properly in select()
concerns.evidence: network/algorithms/round_robin.py:16-28; asyncio.Lock created in __init__ and never used in select()

concerns.title: No Weighted or Adaptive Routing
concerns.description: Only two algorithms available (round_robin, least_active) — no Weighted Round Robin, no Latency-Aware, no Random
concerns.impact: In production you may need channels with different capacities (c1=100rps, c2=10rps) — equal distribution not suitable
concerns.proposed_solution: Add WeightedRoundRobinStrategy and RandomStrategy to algorithms/; expand ILoadBalancerStrategy to support weights
concerns.evidence: network/protocol.py ILoadBalancerStrategy

concerns.title: No Channel Health Probing
concerns.description: AsyncChannelPool does not periodically probe channel health — router may select a dead channel; current health_check() only cleans dead channels without sending pings
concerns.impact: Sending messages to dead channels causes TransportError even with circuit breaker
concerns.proposed_solution: Add PeriodicHealthChecker to monitor channels via send/receive health ping at each interval; update is_healthy in ChannelRef; current health_check() does not check liveness but only cleans dead channels
concerns.evidence: network/adapters/pool_adapter.py:68-87

concerns.title: No Connection Pool Retry / Timeout
concerns.description: AsyncChannelPool.acquire() calls TransportFactory.create_channel() without asyncio.wait_for() — transient failures or connection hangs cause acquire() to hang forever; no connect_retry
concerns.impact: acquire() hanging forever prevents whole pool from working; transient connection failures cause request failure
concerns.proposed_solution: Add connect_timeout (via asyncio.wait_for()) and connect_retry_count to AsyncChannelPool.__init__() and acquire(); severity should be high because pool can hang forever without timeout
concerns.evidence: network/adapters/pool_adapter.py:49; create_channel() unprotected by timeout

concerns.title: RoundRobinStrategy Lock Never Acquired
concerns.description: asyncio.Lock is created in __init__ line 16 but never used in select() lines 18-31 — self._index += 1 is not protected
concerns.impact: self._index += 1 is not thread-safe under concurrency (race conditions); misleading code implies protection that doesn't exist
concerns.proposed_solution: Either use lock properly in select() or remove lock entirely to simplify code
concerns.evidence: network/algorithms/round_robin.py:16; self._lock created but never used

concerns.title: SessionRouterAdapter Hardcodes ChannelState String Values
concerns.description: resolve() line 38 compares channel.state.value in (\active\" \"connecting\") instead of using ChannelState.ACTIVE and ChannelState.CONNECTING"
concerns.impact: If ChannelState enum values change, routing breaks silently without compile-time error
concerns.proposed_solution: Replace string comparison with channel.state in (ChannelState.ACTIVE, ChannelState.CONNECTING)
concerns.evidence: network/adapters/router_adapter.py:38

concerns.title: network/protocol.py Duplicate / Dead Imports
concerns.description: network/protocol.py contains duplicate imports (lines 15-17) and a stale comment \# network/protocol.py (added section only)\""
concerns.impact: Pollutes namespace with unused imports; confuses code readers
concerns.proposed_solution: Remove lines 15-17 (duplicate imports and stale comment)
concerns.evidence: network/protocol.py:15-17

concerns.title: ConfigServiceResolver resolve() Port Filter is No-op
concerns.description: resolve() line 52: [e if e.port != port else ...] returns all endpoints regardless of requested port — no actual filtering occurs
concerns.impact: Calling resolve(name, port=8080) returns endpoints on port 80 without filtering — unexpected behavior
concerns.proposed_solution: Either fix the logic to filter endpoints by port or remove port parameter entirely
concerns.evidence: network/adapters/config_resolver.py:52

concerns.title: AsyncCircuitBreaker No Public record_success/record_failure
concerns.description: _record_success() and _record_failure() are private methods — cannot be called from outside the class (like middleware)
concerns.impact: Cannot implement NET-001 proposed solution because required methods are private; prevents external circuit breaker integration
concerns.proposed_solution: Add public async record_success() and async record_failure() methods that call the private versions
concerns.evidence: network/resilience/circuit_breaker.py:68-88; methods created as async def _record_success and _record_failure (private)

concerns.title: network/__init__.py __all__ misses SessionRouterAdapter
concerns.description: SessionRouterAdapter is imported on line 21 but not included in __all__ (lines 36-59); from network import * will not export it
concerns.impact: Inconsistency between import and export; from network import * won't include SessionRouterAdapter
concerns.proposed_solution: Add SessionRouterAdapter to __all__ list
concerns.evidence: network/__init__.py:21-59

concerns.title: Structured Logging Framework
concerns.description: No unified logging system — logger.debug/info/error scattered without unified schema or structured format
concerns.impact: Debugging and production maintenance difficulty — logs cannot be analyzed automatically
concerns.proposed_solution: Adopt structlog or python-json-logger with unified schema for each layer
concerns.evidence: main.py:13 basicConfig time-only format; every file uses logging.getLogger(__name__); 300+ logging calls across src/ without JSON formatting

concerns.title: OpenTelemetry Distributed Tracing
concerns.description: No tracing linking requests across L3→L8 — correlation_id exists in TransportContext but is not actually used for OTel span/trace
concerns.impact: Difficulty identifying bottlenecks and root cause of failures in multi-layer pipelines
concerns.proposed_solution: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation middleware at L7 entry point (FabricClient.send/receive) and pass context through layers
concerns.evidence: transport/context/transport_context.py:30 carries correlation_id; base.py:55 generates corr-{time_ns()}; 47 grep matches for correlation_id across src/ but no OTel spans

concerns.title: Prometheus Metrics Endpoint
concerns.description: No metrics (message count, latency percentiles, error rate per protocol, active sessions)
concerns.impact: Cannot monitor system health in production or create alerts
concerns.proposed_solution: Add Prometheus client with counters/gauges/histograms for each layer
concerns.evidence: wiring/pipelines/base.py:45 _stats (requests, errors, avg_latency_ms) in memory only; transport/channel/types.py:30 ChannelMetrics exists but not exported; fabric_health.py returns simple status only

concerns.title: Health Check Probes (K8s)
concerns.description: liveness_check/readiness_check exist in CommunicationFabricAssembler but do not actually check underlying components — no circuit breaker, no channel pool health
concerns.impact: /ready may return OK while channel pool or session registry is broken
concerns.proposed_solution: Link liveness_check to event loop health and circuit breaker; link readiness_check to channel pool health_check (pool_adapter.py:68), session registry and each registered pipeline
concerns.evidence: wiring/assembler.py:287-295 liveness only checks {_started, loop_running}; readiness:298-313 only checks pipeline count; adapters/fabric_health.py:28 provides FastAPI endpoints but depends on ICommunicationGateway which lacks liveness/readiness in contract

concerns.title: Audit Logging
concerns.description: No logging of incoming/outgoing messages for audit and compliance purposes
concerns.impact: Cannot track what communicated with what and when in regulated environments
concerns.proposed_solution: Add optional AuditLogger enabled via config — logs source,destination,protocol,session_id,payload_size,timestamp
concerns.evidence: No audit calls or message logging in any pipeline; inbound.py/outbound.py do not log payload metadata

concerns.title: Logger Overhead in Hot Path
concerns.description: PresentationPipeline.encode/decode calls logger.debug on every invocation causing cumulative performance impact on high-throughput paths
concerns.impact: Cumulative performance impact on high-throughput paths
concerns.proposed_solution: Replace logger.debug with isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG) or remove hot-path logging
concerns.evidence: presentation/pipeline.py:104 logger.debug in encode; :127 logger.debug in decode — called for every message

concerns.title: Unified Error Response Schema
concerns.description: No unified error schema across protocols — each protocol handler returns errors in different formats
concerns.impact: Difficult error processing at API gateway and client SDK; cannot analyze failure patterns
concerns.proposed_solution: Create ErrorResponse Pydantic model with code, message, layer, correlation_id, timestamp; unify across all protocol handlers
concerns.evidence: protocols/error_mapper.py exists but not used consistently; PipelineExecutionError (base.py:22) has layer/message/original but not unified as HTTP/gRPC response; logger.error sometimes with exc_info=True sometimes without

concerns.title: Channel Metrics Not Exported
concerns.description: ChannelMetrics (messages_sent, bytes_sent, errors) exists in transport/channel/types.py but not aggregated or exported to any monitoring system
concerns.impact: Cannot monitor individual channel performance or detect memory leaks in pool
concerns.proposed_solution: Aggregate ChannelMetrics via ChannelPool and expose via Prometheus endpoint or health check
concerns.evidence: transport/channel/types.py:30-39 ChannelMetrics dataclass (opened_at, closed_at, messages_sent, bytes_sent, errors); channel.py:45-46 property; but pool_adapter.py does not collect metrics and no exporter exists

concerns.title: ICommunicationGateway Contract Missing Liveness/Readiness
concerns.description: FabricHealthEndpoints calls gateway.liveness_check and gateway.readiness_check but ICommunicationGateway protocol does not declare them — only works by coincidence via structural typing
concerns.impact: Runtime crash if FabricClient interface changes; unclear to new developers
concerns.proposed_solution: Add liveness_check() and readiness_check() to ICommunicationGateway interface with documentation
concerns.evidence: wiring/contracts/communication_gateway.py:45 only health_check declared; adapters/fabric_health.py:56,59 calls gateway.liveness_check and readiness_check as structural typing only

concerns.title: Structured Log Correlation
concerns.description: correlation_id passes through TransportContext in every request but is not injected into logs — cannot link log entries to specific requests
concerns.impact: Debugging requires manual log search; cannot trace request journey across layers
concerns.proposed_solution: Add logging.Filter or setLogRecordFactory to inject correlation_id/session_id from context vars into every log record
concerns.evidence: transport/context/transport_context.py:30 correlation_id in every context; but logging.getLogger(__name__) in every file does not carry context metadata

concerns.title: Session Registry Lacks Observability Metrics
concerns.description: SessionRegistry (session_registry.py) does not log counters for created sessions, ended sessions, evicted sessions, or expired sessions
concerns.impact: Cannot monitor session leaks or usage patterns; difficulty in capacity planning
concerns.proposed_solution: Add internal counters (created, evicted, expired, active) and export via health check or Prometheus
concerns.evidence: session_registry.py: register() only logs; evict_idle() keeps no counters; active_count() only; no total_created, total_evicted

concerns.title: Only JSON Serializer Exists
concerns.description: JsonSerializer exists only and accepts only Pydantic BaseModel — no Protobuf, MsgPack, CBOR, Avro — limits inter-language scenarios
concerns.impact: gRPCRequest exists in request_types.py but no codec implements Protobuf; JSON slower and larger than binary alternatives
concerns.proposed_solution: Add MsgPackSerializer and ProtobufSerializer options; expand SerializerType enum in config; decouple JsonSerializer from BaseModel to support dict/str
concerns.evidence: json_serializer.py:54-57 rejects non-BaseModel in serialize; json_serializer.py:83-86 requires BaseModel subclass in deserialize; request_types.py:49 notes payload converts to Protobuf via future L6 codec

concerns.title: SSEStreamCodec Never Tested with Real Streams
concerns.description: SSEStreamCodec exists but not tested — encode_stream() decode_stream() _parse_sse_frame() never tested with real chunks. Also no tests for other L6 components (JsonSerializer CompressionAdapter PresentationPipeline presentation_provider)
concerns.impact: SSE protocol critical for LLM streaming — parsing errors cause poor user experience; 0% L6 test coverage
concerns.proposed_solution: Create tests: (1) encode->decode roundtrip (2) chunked delivery reassembly (3) multiple data: lines (4) non-JSON fallback (5) empty stream (6) serialization roundtrip for each codec (7) pipeline encode/decode/encode_stream/decode_stream
concerns.evidence: No test_*.py files test presentation/ anywhere under tests/ or any other path

concerns.title: No Adaptive Compression
concerns.description: CompressionAdapter supports only gzip/zstd with fixed algorithm at init — does not dynamically select algorithm based on content type or payload size
concerns.impact: May use zstd for highly-compressible HTML files (slower than gzip) but it doesn't — 20-50% performance opportunity lost by data type
concerns.proposed_solution: Add ContentTypeAnalyzer to read magic bytes or content-type header and select optimal algorithm + level; add Snappy for latency-critical paths
concerns.evidence: compression.py:33-39 only two algorithms with fixed values (algorithm + level) that never change dynamically; pipeline.py:70-74 _should_compress checks size only and does not affect algorithm selection

concerns.title: No Charset Normalization
concerns.description: charset exists in PipelineConfig (utf-8 default) but only used in encode_stream bypass mode (line 142). No automatic encoding conversion when receiving Shift-JIS or Latin-1 data
concerns.impact: If client sends Shift-JIS or Latin-1, pipeline does not convert — garbage may reach L5/Session; sse_stream_codec uses errors='replace' causing silent data loss
concerns.proposed_solution: Add chardet detection + iconv/conversion between source encoding and utf-8; make config-disablable; link charset to sse_stream_codec decode
concerns.evidence: pipeline.py:30 charset=default('utf-8') but never used in normal encode/decode; pipeline.py:142 only used in bypass encode_stream; sse_stream_codec.py:81 errors='replace' hides encoding problems

concerns.title: StreamCodec and Serializer Coexistence Ambiguous
concerns.description: PresentationPipeline accepts both serializer and stream_codec but unclear which is used in encode_stream/decode_stream — encode_stream logic (lines 148-161) completely bypasses serializer if codec exists. decode_stream (line 183) passes target_type=Any to serializer.deserialize causing crash with JsonSerializer
concerns.impact: Wrong linking may cause double-serialization or skip compression entirely; decode_stream crashes with JsonSerializer when using serializer-only without codec due to target_type=Any
concerns.proposed_solution: Document clear strategy: (1) if stream_codec exists -> responsible for framing and serializer used inside codec (2) if serializer only -> stream_codec wraps serialize for each item (3) fix target_type=Any in decode_stream to avoid crash with JsonSerializer
concerns.evidence: pipeline.py:148-161 encode_stream completely bypasses serializer.serialize(obj) when codec truthy (line 152); pipeline.py:183 decode_stream passes target_type=Any but JsonSerializer.deserialize requires BaseModel subclass

concerns.title: No Stream-Level Compression
concerns.description: PresentationPipeline.encode_stream does not compress individual frames — self.compressor is never called in the stream encoding path (lines 138-161). However decode_stream (line 177) attempts self.compressor.decompress(frame) on incoming frames. This asymmetry causes stream decode failure
concerns.impact: SSE and streaming APIs send uncompressed text — low transfer efficiency; asymmetry: encode_stream never compresses but decode_stream attempts decompression
concerns.proposed_solution: Add per-chunk compression in encode_stream with algorithm declared in SSE header or metadata; sync encode with decode
concerns.evidence: pipeline.py:138-161 encode_stream never uses self.compressor; pipeline.py:177 decode_stream attempts self.compressor.decompress(frame) but encode_stream never compressed frames at all

concerns.title: JsonSerializer BaseModel Constraint Breaks decode_stream
concerns.description: JsonSerializer.serialize rejects non-BaseModel and JsonSerializer.deserialize requires target_type as BaseModel subclass — this causes crash in decode_stream (line 183) which passes target_type=Any. Prevents passing plain dict/list/str through pipeline
concerns.impact: PresentationPipeline.decode_stream crashes when using serializer-only without codec because target_type=Any fails issubclass check in json_serializer.py:83; Cannot pass plain dict/list/str through pipeline
concerns.proposed_solution: Modify JsonSerializer to accept dict via json.dumps/loads while keeping BaseModel support via model_dump_json/model_validate_json; fix pipeline.py:183 to use type() or pass None with fallback
concerns.evidence: json_serializer.py:54-57 if not isinstance(obj, BaseModel): raise; json_serializer.py:83-86 if not issubclass(target_type, BaseModel): raise; pipeline.py:183 yield self.serializer.deserialize(data, target_type=Any)

concerns.title: Zero Unit Tests for Entire L6 Layer
concerns.description: No unit or integration tests for JsonSerializer SSEStreamCodec CompressionAdapter PresentationPipeline presentation_provider nor any test file covering presentation/
concerns.impact: 0% test coverage for any codec or pipeline — simple changes may break serialization or streaming undetected; PRS-002 covers SSE only but issue is broader
concerns.proposed_solution: Create comprehensive suite: (1) JsonSerializer roundtrip with BaseModel/dict (2) SSEStreamCodec encode->decode with real chunks and delay (3) CompressionAdapter gzip+zstd roundtrip + auto-detect (4) PresentationPipeline encode/decode/encode_stream/decode_stream with all combinations (5) presentation_provider for each direction (6) bypass mode tests (7) edge cases: empty/None/malformed/large data
concerns.evidence: glob search for test*.py under src/ returns nothing for presentation; tests/ contains only test_live_protocol_switch.py which does not test L6 directly; .docs/usage_integration_examples/tests/ does not test any codec directly

concerns.title: SSEStreamCodec Drops Event/ID/Retry Fields
concerns.description: SSEStreamCodec processes only data: fields and ignores event: id: retry: — encode_stream does not emit them decode_stream does not parse them — breaks EventSource API compatibility
concerns.impact: Incompatible with browsers and SSE clients; cannot route different events (chat.completion vs error) via SSE event types
concerns.proposed_solution: Add support for event:/id:/retry: in encode_stream (parameters event_type/event_id) and decode_stream (_parse_sse_frame collects all fields in unified dict with data key) maintaining backward compatibility
concerns.evidence: sse_stream_codec.py:49 emits only 'data: ...\\n\\n' without event:/id:/retry:; sse_stream_codec.py:86-93 _parse_sse_frame collects only lines.startswith('data:') and ignores other SSE boundaries

concerns.title: Bypass Mode encode_stream Crashes on Non-Serializable Objects
concerns.description: In bypass mode encode_stream (line 142) attempts chunk.encode(charset) for any non-bytes non-str — crashes if chunk is dict/list/int/None
concerns.impact: If INPROCESS direction used with encode_stream on non-bytes stream (e.g. dict chunks) -> AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'encode' — incomplete bypass implementation
concerns.dependencies: pipeline.py:141-143 yield chunk if isinstance(chunk, bytes) else chunk.encode(self.config.charset) assumes non-bytes are always str
concerns.proposed_solution: Add isinstance(chunk, str) guard before .encode() call. For non-str/non-bytes types, use json.dumps() or str() as fallback. Raise clear PresentationError only if all conversions fail
concerns.notes: presentation_L6

concerns.title: presentation_provider bypass_inprocess Parameter is Dead Config
concerns.description: The bypass_inprocess parameter in build_presentation_pipeline does not affect PresentationPipeline behavior — pipeline decides bypass itself based on Direction.INPROCESS in _is_bypass(). The conditional branch (lines 75-77) only does debug log
concerns.impact: Developer confusion: parameter exists in public API but does nothing; users think disabling it prevents bypass but pipeline bypasses anyway
concerns.dependencies: presentation_provider.py:75-77 if bypass_inprocess and direction == INPROCESS does nothing; pipeline.py:64-68 _is_bypass() decides bypass autonomously without external config
concerns.proposed_solution: Remove bypass_inprocess parameter from build_presentation_pipeline entirely or wire it into PipelineConfig as real flag that _is_bypass() checks centralizing all bypass decisions in pipeline itself

concerns.title: No Metrics or Monitoring Hooks in Pipeline
concerns.description: PresentationPipeline contains no callbacks/events/metrics hooks to measure compression ratio serialization latency throughput or error rates - cannot monitor L6 performance in production
concerns.impact: No production visibility on L6 performance — cannot detect serialization bottlenecks or silent compression failures; relies only on logging.debug
concerns.dependencies: pipeline contains no metrics/counter/timer; no Prometheus/OpenTelemetry integration anywhere in pipeline or codecs
concerns.proposed_solution: Add optional Counter/Timer hooks in pipeline.encode/decode/encode_stream/decode_stream measuring: (1) pre/post compression size (2) serialization duration (3) errors per codec (4) bytes processed; use structlog or OpenTelemetry spans with optional injection via PipelineConfig

concerns.title: SSEStreamCodec encode_stream Crashes on Non-Serializable Objects
concerns.description: SSEStreamCodec.encode_stream calls json.dumps(obj) directly at line 45 on non-bytes non-str objects when auto_json=True — raises TypeError if obj contains non-serializable types (Callable set datetime complex)
concerns.impact: Streaming crashes on non-JSON-serializable objects; InProcessRequest.target excluded (exclude=True) but arbitrary objects outside control
concerns.dependencies: sse_stream_codec.py:44-45 if self.auto_json: data_str = json.dumps(obj, ensure_ascii=False); no str(obj)/repr(obj) fallback
concerns.proposed_solution: Wrap json.dumps in try/except TypeError with str(obj)/repr(obj) fallback; log serialization failure at warning level; auto_json=False prevents this error

concerns.title: decode_stream Passes Decompressed Data Back to Codec Without Re-Compression
concerns.description: decode_stream (line 177) decompresses frame then passes decompressed data to codec.decode_stream — but encode_stream never compressed frames (PRS-006). This asymmetry causes stream decode failure
concerns.impact: If stream compressed at message level (encode/decode) and passed via streaming, decode_stream attempts decompression of frame that may not be compressed — may produce corrupted data or raise decompress error
concerns.dependencies: pipeline.py:177 data = self.compressor.decompress(frame) if self.compressor and self._is_compressed(frame) else frame — _is_compressed guard prevents damage but relies on magic bytes that may coincidentally match start of uncompressed data
concerns.proposed_solution: Add explicit X-Compression header in first SSE frame (e.g. X-Compression: gzip/zstd/none) so decode_stream knows whether stream is compressed; fully synchronize encode_stream and decode_stream on compression behavior (both compress or neither)

concerns.title: CompressionAdapter Lacks Edge Case Handling for Empty/Truncated Data
concerns.description: CompressionAdapter.compress/decompress does not handle: (1) empty data b'' (2) truncated data (3) corrupted data with correct magic bytes but invalid content — gzip/zstd raise uncaught exceptions
concerns.impact: Empty data produces non-zero compressed size (gzip produces 20 bytes for empty input) causing bloat; truncated data causes OSError/gzip.Error/zstd.Error uncaught by PresentationError
concerns.dependencies: compression.py:41-52 compress does not handle b'' as edge case; compression.py:54-67 decompress does not handle truncated/corrupted data except generic try/except
concerns.proposed_solution: Add is_empty = len(data) == 0 -> return b'' directly in compress/decompress without algorithm; add explicit catch for gzip.BadGzipFile and zstandard.ZstdError before generic except

concerns.title: gRPC Handler Structural Incompatibility with ABC
concerns.description: GrpcProtocolHandler exists in protocols/grpc_handler.py with full implementation (unary, streaming, serve, close) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler. Method signatures (handle_outbound/serve) differ completely from prepare_outbound/process_inbound in ABC. Cannot verify gRPC protocol contract via IDE static analysis; LSP broken.
concerns.impact: Cannot verify gRPC protocol contract via IDE static analysis; replacing it breaks LSP
concerns.proposed_solution: Make GrpcProtocolHandler inherit IProtocolHandler; unify function names or update ABC to match real protocol signatures
concerns.evidence: protocols/grpc_handler.py:30-48
concerns.notes: grpc_handler.py exists and implemented — not missing_component

concerns.title: GraphQL Handler Structural Incompatibility with ABC
concerns.description: GraphQLProtocolHandler exists in protocols/graphql_handler.py with full implementation (queries, subscriptions, errors) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler. Method signatures (handle_inbound/handle_outbound) differ entirely from ABC. Cannot verify contract via IDE static analysis.
concerns.impact: Cannot verify GraphQL handler meets contract; replacing it breaks LSP
concerns.proposed_solution: Make GraphQLProtocolHandler inherit IProtocolHandler; unify interface while preserving GraphQL semantic accuracy (query/variables/operationName)
concerns.evidence: protocols/graphql_handler.py:25-39
concerns.notes: graphql_handler.py exists and implemented — not missing_component

concerns.title: No Dedicated WebSocket Protocol Handler
concerns.description: No standalone protocols/websocket_handler.py file exists. http_handler.py contains _handle_websocket_request (lines 216-227) within ASGI app but is not a standalone handler. ProtocolType.WEBSOCKET and ProtocolErrorMapper._to_websocket() exist. Cannot use WebSocket as independent protocol — depends on HTTP handler for WS lifecycle.
concerns.impact: Cannot use WebSocket as independent protocol — depends on HTTP handler for WS
concerns.proposed_solution: Create standalone WebSocketProtocolHandler inheriting IProtocolHandler; extract WS logic from http_handler; support text/binary frames, ping/pong, close codes
concerns.evidence: protocols/http_handler.py:216-227 — only embedded within HTTP handler
concerns.notes: http_handler.py handles WebSocket only within ASGI app

concerns.title: Webhook Handler Structural Incompatibility with ABC
concerns.description: WebhookProtocolHandler exists in protocols/webhook_handler.py with full implementation (HMAC-SHA256 signature verification, idempotency, replay protection) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler. Missing process_outbound_response() and close(). Resource leak: idempotency_store grows unbounded.
concerns.impact: Cannot verify protocol contract; resource leak (no close method)
concerns.proposed_solution: Make WebhookProtocolHandler inherit IProtocolHandler; add process_outbound_response() and close(); add periodic idempotency_store cleanup
concerns.evidence: protocols/webhook_handler.py:27-47
concerns.notes: webhook_handler.py exists and implemented — not missing_component

concerns.title: Local IPC Handler Structural Incompatibility with ABC
concerns.description: LocalIpcProtocolHandler exists in protocols/local_ipc_handler.py with full implementation (outbound, inbound, streaming, peer verification) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler. Missing process_outbound_response(). Type safety not guaranteed.
concerns.impact: Cannot verify protocol contract; type safety not guaranteed
concerns.proposed_solution: Make LocalIpcProtocolHandler inherit IProtocolHandler; add process_outbound_response()
concerns.evidence: protocols/local_ipc_handler.py:30-51
concerns.notes: local_ipc_handler.py exists and implemented — not missing_component

concerns.title: IProtocolHandler ABC Not Inherited by Any Handler
concerns.description: protocols/protocol.py defines IProtocolHandler(ABC) with 4 abstract methods (prepare_outbound, process_inbound, process_outbound_response, close) but NONE of the 7 handlers inherit it. Contract is structural-only; IDE static analysis catches zero violations; LSP completely broken.
concerns.impact: IDE static analysis cannot catch errors; LSP broken; any new handler may miss essential functions
concerns.proposed_solution: Make all 7 handlers inherit IProtocolHandler; evaluate if ABC methods need updating to accommodate protocol differences (each protocol has unique signature)
concerns.evidence: protocols/protocol.py:9-35 plus all 7 handler files
concerns.notes: Problem broader than HTTP only — all 7 handlers lack inheritance

concerns.title: ErrorMapper Missing Dedicated Mapping LOCAL_IPC/GRAPHQL/WEBHOOK
concerns.description: ProtocolErrorMapper.map() defines ProtocolType.LOCAL_IPC, GRAPHQL, WEBHOOK in Enum but no _to_local_ipc, _to_graphql, _to_webhook functions. The match statement (lines 70-76) falls through to case _: return cls._to_http(cat). IPC/Webhook/GraphQL errors translated as HTTP.
concerns.impact: IPC errors appear as HTTP 500; GraphQL errors get HTTP headers; inaccurate retryable hints
concerns.proposed_solution: Add _to_local_ipc, _to_graphql, _to_webhook to ProtocolErrorMapper; add retryable constants per protocol; update match statement
concerns.evidence: protocols/error_mapper.py:70-76 case _: fallback to HTTP
concerns.notes: All handlers actually use ProtocolErrorMapper — issue is mapper itself incomplete

concerns.title: All Handler Methods Mismatch IProtocolHandler Signatures
concerns.description: IProtocolHandler ABC assumes uniform prepare_outbound(payload, ctx) but real handlers need different parameters (http: method/path/headers/payload; cli: command/env/payload; graphql: query/variables/operationName; inprocess: target_override). Uniform contract is architecturally wrong. No handler can substitute another; ABC is currently useless as unified contract; protocol_provider falsely returns IProtocolHandler types.
concerns.impact: No handler can replace another; ABC currently useless as unified contract; protocol_provider returns false IProtocolHandler
concerns.proposed_solution: Redesign IProtocolHandler: either (a) make it wide ProtocolHandlerInterface with Generic Types per-protocol, or (b) reduce ABC to only shared close() method and let each protocol define its own signature freely
concerns.evidence: protocols/protocol.py vs all handler signatures
concerns.notes: Problem deeper than HTTP only — affects all handlers

concerns.title: No Factory Module in protocols/ Package
concerns.description: No factory.py exists inside protocols/. Handler construction logic lives in wiring/factories/protocol_provider.py separate from protocol package. Breaks encapsulation and adding a new handler requires modifying two separate locations.
concerns.impact: Adding a new handler requires knowledge of two separate locations; registration logic not consolidated
concerns.proposed_solution: Move build_protocol_handler logic from protocol_provider.py to protocols/factory.py; re-export via __init__.py; make protocol_provider.py delegate to protocols.factory.build_protocol_handler
concerns.evidence: src/protocols/ has no factory.py vs src/wiring/factories/protocol_provider.py:23-93
concerns.notes: Architectural improvement — not blocking

concerns.title: Multiple Handlers Missing close() and process_outbound_response
concerns.description: InProcessProtocolHandler (no close, no process_outbound_response), CliProtocolHandler (no close, no process_outbound_response), GraphQLProtocolHandler (no close, no process_outbound_response), WebhookProtocolHandler (no close, no process_outbound_response) — only 3 of 7 handlers meet ABC contract fully.
concerns.impact: Resource leaks on close for inprocess/cli/graphql/webhook; inconsistent lifecycle management across 7 protocols
concerns.proposed_solution: Add close() (to release resources) and process_outbound_response() (to process outbound responses) to each missing handler; provide default implementation in ABC
concerns.evidence: multiple handler files
concerns.notes: Includes 4 handlers missing lifecycle functions

concerns.title: CliProtocolHandler and WebhookProtocolHandler Permit Resource Leak
concerns.description: CliProtocolHandler and WebhookProtocolHandler have no close() method — no resources released. CliProtocolHandler opens stdin/stdout in handle_inbound (line 113) but has no close() to release them. WebhookProtocolHandler accumulates idempotency_store entries indefinitely (only clears at 100k entries). Both leak resources in long-running applications.
concerns.impact: stdin/stdout remain open; idempotency_store grows unbounded causing memory leak
concerns.proposed_solution: Add close() to CliProtocolHandler to release stdin/stdout handles. Improve WebhookProtocolHandler.clear_expired_idempotency() with TTL-based partial eviction instead of full clear at 100k threshold
concerns.evidence: protocols/cli_handler.py protocols/webhook_handler.py
concerns.notes: Production risk for long-running applications

concerns.title: ErrorMapper Lacks Retryable Constants for LOCAL_IPC and GRAPHQL and WEBHOOK
concerns.description: _RETRYABLE sets exist only for HTTP (line 49: {429,500,502,503,504}), CLI (line 50: {1,2}), and gRPC (line 51: {4,14}). LOCAL_IPC, GRAPHQL, and WEBHOOK have no retryable status lists. Even if we add _to_* methods, retryable flags would be inaccurate, causing unnecessary retries or missed retry opportunities.
concerns.impact: Retryable hints for L5 will be wrong for new protocols causing unnecessary or missed retries
concerns.proposed_solution: Add _LOCAL_IPC_RETRYABLE and _GRAPHQL_RETRYABLE and _WEBHOOK_RETRYABLE constants; document criteria per status
concerns.evidence: protocols/error_mapper.py:49-51
concerns.notes: Requires PRT-007 first

concerns.title: No TLS/mTLS Support
concerns.description: BaseTransporter sends plain bytes without any encryption — all channels (TCP,UDS,IPC,WS,Subprocess) are unencrypted. Applications requiring secure network connections (A2A over internet, external gRPC, external webhooks) are fully exposed.
concerns.impact: All traffic is exposed — critical security breach in any non-isolated production environment
concerns.proposed_solution: Add TLSConfig to TransportConfig with cert/key/ca paths; modify TCPTransporter, WebSocketTransporter, SubprocessTransporter to use ssl_context in asyncio.open_connection()
concerns.evidence: transport/tcp.py:64-65: asyncio.open_connection() without ssl_context; transport/websocket.py:63-64: same issue; transport/transport_config.py: no TLS options; .obsolete/grpc.py had full TLS but is disabled

concerns.title: No Authentication/Authorization
concerns.description: No authentication layer — any process can connect to FabricService and send/receive messages unrestricted. No JWT, API keys, OAuth, or identity model.
concerns.impact: Any process on same machine or network can use fabric without restrictions — data leakage and unauthorized access
concerns.proposed_solution: Add AuthToken to FabricClient.send() carrying JWT/API key; FabricService validates token on receive(); add AuthConfig to AppConfig
concerns.evidence: wiring/assembler.py:68-81: FabricClient.send() lacks auth parameter; protocols/http_handler.py:37-55: HttpProtocolHandler lacks api_key/token; transport/base.py:124: ErrorType.PERMANENT references 'unauthorized' but no implementation exists

concerns.title: No Message-Level Encryption
concerns.description: PresentationPipeline only compresses (gzip/zstd) and does not encrypt — payload is exposed even with TLS because TLS protects transport only not storage/middleware.
concerns.impact: In multi-tenant or middleware scenarios, data may be visible to other applications or in logs
concerns.proposed_solution: Add optional EncryptionStage to PresentationPipeline using AES-GCM or libsodium via new IEncryptor protocol
concerns.evidence: presentation/pipeline.py:84-134: encode/decode does not encrypt, only serialize + compress; presentation/protocol.py: no IEncryptor protocol; presentation/codecs/: no encrypt/decrypt codec

concerns.title: No Secrets Management
concerns.description: No secure storage for TLS certs, API keys, shared_secrets, or tokens. Everything is read from plain YAML or passed as string in code. Production requires Vault/AKV/K8s Secrets.
concerns.impact: Keys and credentials will leak in production — all keys are in plaintext YAML or code
concerns.proposed_solution: Support env vars ($ENV_VAR) in ConfigLoader + optional Vault/HashiCorp/Azure Key Vault adapter as config source; encrypt sensitive fields in YAML
concerns.evidence: wiring/config/loader.py:67-139: load_config reads YAML only without os.getenv or vault integration; wiring/factories/protocol_provider.py:78: shared_secret=entry.endpoint — using endpoint as secret (security breach); transport/config/transport_config.py: no secrets config
concerns.notes: SEC-004 differs from SEC-008: SEC-004 focuses on storage and secret management, SEC-008 focuses on secret leakage in endpoint

concerns.title: No Input Validation / Content Sanitization
concerns.description: PresentationPipeline and protocol handlers perform no validation or sanitization on payloads. Data flows from network to application unchecked — Injection, XXE, Prototype Pollution attacks possible.
concerns.impact: Malicious payloads may reach handlers or core application without filtering — injection vulnerabilities and data integrity issues
concerns.proposed_solution: Add ValidationStage to PresentationPipeline with Pydantic schema validation or allowlist; sanitization for input/output in protocol handlers
concerns.evidence: presentation/pipeline.py:84-134: encode/decode does not inspect content; protocols/http_handler.py: no validation on body; protocols/graphql_handler.py: passes query directly to executor

concerns.title: No Security Audit Logging
concerns.description: No security audit trail (logs) — failed auth attempts, sensitive config changes, and unauthorized access are not logged. Breach detection and forensics are impossible. Violates SOC2/ISO27001/HIPAA.
concerns.impact: Cannot track security events — violates SOC2/ISO27001 and HIPAA requirements in regulated environments
concerns.proposed_solution: Add SecurityAuditLogger with events: AUTH_SUCCESS, AUTH_FAIL, CONFIG_CHANGE, ACCESS_DENIED; emit audit events in FabricClient and ConfigLoader
concerns.evidence: transport/base.py:129-134: build_retry_decision_hook unrelated to security; wiring/assembler.py: FabricClient has no audit; fastapi_router.py: no audit log for authentication

concerns.title: No Rate Limiting / DoS Protection
concerns.description: No protection against DoS attacks or sudden request bursts. Circuit breaker exists but focuses on service failures not attack protection.
concerns.impact: Resources (CPU, memory, connections) may be exhausted via rapid consecutive requests — service outage risk
concerns.proposed_solution: Add RateLimiter (token-bucket or sliding window) to ChannelPool and SessionDispatcher; configure max requests per session/channel
concerns.evidence: network/adapters/pool_adapter.py: no rate limiting; network/resilience/circuit_breaker.py: focuses on destination failure not attack protection; transport/factory.py: no rate limiting config

concerns.title: Insecure Credential Handling (endpoint as shared_secret)
concerns.description: In protocol_provider.py, entry.endpoint (URL/path field) is used as shared_secret for WebhookProtocolHandler. This is a security leak: endpoint is also used in logs and routing, exposing the secret.
concerns.impact: shared_secret appears in logs, debugging, and monitoring — immediate webhook HMAC breach risk
concerns.proposed_solution: Add separate shared_secret field to ProtocolEntryConfig in ConfigLoader; do not reuse endpoint for security purposes
concerns.evidence: wiring/factories/protocol_provider.py:78: shared_secret=entry.endpoint if entry.endpoint and len(entry.endpoint) > 10 else None — using endpoint (URL!) as secret; wiring/config/loader.py:52: ProtocolEntryConfig lacks shared_secret field

concerns.title: No Certificate / PKI Management Infrastructure
concerns.description: No certificate management infrastructure — no auto-generation, renewal, revocation, or CA chain validation. Even with TLS added, there is no system to manage keys and certificates.
concerns.impact: Certificates will expire or be compromised without centralized management — service interruption or security vulnerabilities
concerns.proposed_solution: Add CertificateManager with auto-renewal (Let's Encrypt/cert-manager), CA bundle management, cert rotation hooks; integrate with Vault/AKV for key storage
concerns.evidence: transport/config/transport_config.py: no cert/key/ca field; wiring/config/loader.py: no PKI config; .obsolete/grpc.py:27-37 (disabled) had root_certificates, private_key, certificate_chain but dead code
concerns.notes: TLS/mTLS requirements incomplete without PKI management

concerns.title: ISessionLifecycle Not Integrated with SessionDispatcher
concerns.description: ISessionLifecycle.open() is never called during SessionDispatcher.dispatch() or SessionRegistry.register(). Sessions are created without lifecycle activation. lifecycle.close() is called indirectly via registry.unregister() -> _on_session_end() -> coordinator.close() -> lifecycle.close() but there is no direct open() path from the delivery path. Additionally, SessionRegistry.register() is responsible for session startup but does not call lifecycle.open().
concerns.impact: Sessions have no default lifetime — ttl_seconds and idle_timeout_seconds are never enabled because SessionConfig from ISessionLifecycle is never passed through register()
concerns.proposed_solution: Inject ISessionLifecycle into SessionDispatcher or SessionRegistry; call .open() with SessionConfig during register() and .refresh() per message and .close() on release_session(). Best solution: add lifecycle to SessionRegistry.register() instead of SessionDispatcher because it owns session startup.
concerns.evidence: session/session_registry.py:73-78 does not call lifecycle.open(); session/session_dispatcher.py:64-96 does not call lifecycle.open()/refresh()/close()

concerns.title: ICheckpointSync Adapters Exist But Not Wired in Production Assembly
concerns.description: Previous description was 'Not Backed by Storage' but this is inaccurate. Three complete implementations exist: CLISessionAdapter (files), InProcessSessionAdapter (memory), NetworkSessionAdapter (external storage). But the wiring in assembler.py never passes checkpoint_sync to SessionRegistry at all, so persist() will never be called in production. The gap is in wiring, not implementation.
concerns.impact: No session persistence — any crash loses all checkpoints and sessions in production despite ready adapters
concerns.proposed_solution: Add checkpoint_sync to _build_session_registry() in assembler.py: pass appropriate instance (e.g. InProcessSessionAdapter or CLISessionAdapter) based on transport_type in config.
concerns.evidence: wiring/assembler.py:241-246 does not pass checkpoint_sync; session/adapters/ has three complete implementations but not wired

concerns.title: Session Timeout Not Enforced (TTL expiry missing)
concerns.description: SessionRegistry has evict_idle() that checks only idle_timeout and does not check ttl_seconds (created_at + ttl). Sessions that were recently active but whose total age exceeded TTL will never be evicted. Plus eviction_interval default (60s) means sessions stay up to 60 seconds past expiration.
concerns.impact: Memory leak with accumulation of expired-but-active sessions.
concerns.proposed_solution: Extend evict_idle() to apply two conditions: (1) idle_timeout and (2) TTL — entry.created_at + entry.ttl < now. Reduce eviction_interval to 10-30s default.
concerns.evidence: session/session_registry.py:106-124 only checks idle_timeout not TTL; session_registry.py:47 default_ttl=3600 defined but not applied in eviction

concerns.title: No Distributed Session Coordination
concerns.description: SessionRegistry/ SessionDispatcher are purely local — sessions cannot be shared across multiple fabric instances.
concerns.impact: Horizontal scaling impossible — each instance starts with fresh sessions and state cannot be distributed
concerns.proposed_solution: Add RedisSessionRegistry and RedisSessionDispatcher persisting session state in Redis with serialization via pickle/json; update ServiceDiscovery to point to Redis.
concerns.evidence: session/session_registry.py uses plain dict only

concerns.title: No Tests for Any Session Component
concerns.description: No tests exist in tests/ or session/. All components (SessionRegistry, SessionDispatcher, SessionCoordinator, all three adapters, hooks, factory) are untested.
concerns.impact: Cannot rely on any session logic — critical bugs (SES-007/008/009) will go undetected
concerns.proposed_solution: Create unit and integration tests: (1) register/dispatch/resolve (2) failover with frozen DispatchContext (3) coordinator.handle_success/failure (4) full adapter lifecycle (5) eviction idle + TTL.
concerns.evidence: no test_session*.py files found in entire project tree; session_registry.py, session_dispatcher.py, coordinator.py, adapters/*.py all untested

concerns.title: PrunePolicy Never Applied from Orchestration Layer
concerns.description: Some adapters implement prune() (CLI calls it in close(), InProcess supports it) but SessionRegistry._on_session_end() never calls prune() at all. Instead it would call persist() which doesn't exist in ICheckpointSync. No auto-prune or manual trigger exists from orchestration layer. Checkpoints accumulate without limit.
concerns.impact: Checkpoints accumulate without limit — unbounded storage consumption in CLI and Network adapters
concerns.proposed_solution: Add checkpoint.prune() call inside _on_session_end() after persist() (or instead of it). Add scheduled prune task in SessionRegistry per PrunePolicy.
concerns.evidence: session/session_registry.py:172-181 does not call prune(); session/ICheckpointSync.py:44 PrunePolicy supported but unused from orchestration layer

concerns.title: TTL Expiry Not Checked in SessionRegistry.evict_idle()
concerns.description: SessionRegistry.evict_idle() only checks idle_timeout (idle time) and does not check TTL (created_at + ttl < now). Sessions that are recent but whose total age exceeded TTL will never be evicted. This makes default_ttl ineffective.
concerns.impact: memory leak: old-age sessions that are not idle stay forever; default_ttl becomes useless
concerns.proposed_solution: Add TTL condition to evict_idle(): if now - entry.created_at > entry.ttl -> evict. Merge both conditions in same pass.

concerns.title: No Zero-Copy Path for Large Payloads

concerns.proposed_solution: Add optional LargePayloadHandler that detects >threshold and reads/writes from temporary file or mmap instead of RAM; pass file descriptor via UDS as immediate solution for same-device

concerns.title: UDS Newline-Delimited Framing Fragile for Binary
concerns.description: UDS uses newline-delimited framing (b\\n) to delimit messages — any payload containing \\n within data will truncate the message. TCP uses length-prefixed safely while UDS does not
concerns.impact: Binary payloads cannot be transferred safely via UDS — silent errors may cause corrupted messages
concerns.proposed_solution: Change UDS to length-prefixed framing (like TCP: struct.pack + length header) with backward compatibility or explicit framing config
